text: Creates and fires a projectile from a player.
The projectile is normally fired from the player's position, but due to the weapon being held off-center it may require a fake position for where the projectile appears to be fired from.
deprecated: Name is deprecated, use uuid instead
params: The visual start position in third-person. (Defaults to position)
The visual start position in first-person. (Defaults to position)
The number of ticks before firing. (Defaults to 0)

text: Removes projectiles created by a given source. The source world must exist.
params: The world that is the source of the projectile.
The number of ticks before destroying the projectile. (Defaults to 0)

text: Removes projectiles created by a given source. The source player must exist.
params: The player that is the source of the projectile.
The number of ticks before destroying the projectile. (Defaults to 0)

text: Removes projectiles created by a given character source. Can remove projectiles from a source that doesn't exist, in this case a world to remove from is required.
params: The Character that is the source of the projectile.
The number of ticks before destroying the projectile. (Defaults to 0)
The world the projectiles should be removed from. (Defaults to same world as source.)

text: Calculate the ballistic arc of a projectile. There are two potential solutions to the problem.
One with a low fire angle and one with a high fire angle. Solutions can be nil if no solution is found.
params: The position the projectile is fired from.
The position the projectile should hit.
The fire velocity of the projectile.
return: The low angle solution; The high angle solution.
